title: acer saccharum var. acuminatum
intro: Acer saccharum var. acuminatum, also known as the white sugar maple or mountain sugar maple, is a variety of the sugar maple tree, native to the mountainous regions of eastern North America. The bark and inner bark of this plant contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, which makes it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Acer saccharum var. acuminatum include relief from pain and inflammation, and it has also been traditionally used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the bark or inner bark in hot water or a solvent, and then straining and drying the resulting liquid.
